step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("cells most commonly affected in glaucomatous optic atrophy")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the gathered evidence, all sources consistently highlight that retinal ganglion cells (ganglion cells) are significantly impacted in various forms of glaucoma, including primary open-angle glaucoma (POAG), pigment dispersion glaucoma, juvenile glaucoma, and normal tension glaucoma (NTG). Multiple studies describe the direct correlation between ganglion cell loss and the characteristic morphological changes observed in the optic nerve heads, such as increased cup-to-disk ratios and thinning of the retinal nerve fiber layer. Furthermore, the evidence underscores the critical role of ganglion cells in maintaining proper visual signal transmission from photoreceptors to the brain, making them prime targets for damage in glaucomatous processes. Thus, I reaffirm my agreement with option C being correct.

Tool Call: retrieve("cells most commonly affected in glaucomatous optic atrophy")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the gathered evidence, all sources consistently highlight that retinal ganglion cells (ganglion cells) are significantly impacted in various forms of glaucoma, including primary open-angle glaucoma (POAG), pigment dispersion glaucoma, juvenile glaucoma, and normal tension glaucoma (NTG). Multiple studies describe the direct correlation between ganglion cell loss and the characteristic morphological changes observed in the optic nerve heads, such as increased cup-to-disk ratios and thinning of the retinal nerve fiber layer. Furthermore, the evidence underscores the critical role of ganglion cells in maintaining proper visual signal transmission from photoreceptors to the brain, making them prime targets for damage in glaucomatous processes. Thus, I reaffirm my agreement with option C being correct.
\boxed{C}
